page: Vultr vbm-64c-1536gb-amd is a bare metal server belonging to the Bare Metal NVMe family. It is equipped with an AMD EPYC 9354 processor featuring 128 physical cores and 128 vCPUs, operating at a clock speed of 3.2 GHz. The system architecture is x86_64, and the CPU threads per core ratio is 1.0, indicating dedicated physical execution resources without hyperthreading.

This server configuration includes 1536.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 12.0 GB per core. Local storage is provided by a 480 GB NVMe SSD. The server does not include any graphics processing units and comes with one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Given the bare metal deployment model and the high density of both dedicated AMD EPYC cores and system memory, this server offers significant resource isolation and raw compute capacity. The 1:12 vCPU-to-RAM ratio positions it as a memory-optimized platform. It is suitable for memory-intensive workloads, large-scale databases, virtualization hosting, and enterprise applications requiring dedicated physical hardware without virtualization overhead.
description: Vultr vbm-64c-1536gb-amd is a dedicated bare metal server from the Bare Metal NVMe family, powered by an AMD EPYC 9354 processor. It features 128 physical cores and 128 vCPUs running at 3.2 GHz on the x86_64 architecture, with a 1.0 thread-per-core configuration ensuring fully dedicated execution resources. The server is equipped with 1536.0 GB of RAM, providing a substantial 12.0 GB of memory per core, alongside a 480 GB NVMe SSD for local storage. With zero GPUs and one complimentary public IPv4 address, this high-density hardware profile is designed for resource-intensive enterprise applications. The combination of massive memory capacity and dedicated multi-core processing makes this server highly suitable for large-scale databases, virtualization infrastructure, and memory-heavy enterprise workloads requiring physical hardware isolation.

tagline: A dedicated bare metal server featuring 128 AMD EPYC cores and 1.5 TB of RAM for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
